name: Side Plank
resistance_profile: Anti-lateral-flexion core hold: resist gravity pulling the hips toward the floor — trains the obliques and lateral core to brace. A no-equipment rotation/anti-rotation option.
cues: Stack the feet and hips in a straight line
Lift the hips and brace the side of your core
execution_steps: Lie on your side with your forearm on the floor and your elbow under your shoulder.
Stack your feet and legs and line your body up straight.
Lift your hips off the floor so your body forms a straight line from head to feet.
Brace the side of your core and hold for time, keeping your hips from sagging.
Repeat on the other side for equal time.
good_when: You want to train the obliques and lateral core with no equipment.
You're a beginner building anti-lateral-flexion stability.
You need a low-back-friendly side-core option.
bad_when: You want direct ab growth (use loaded crunches).
The bottom shoulder is painful under load (regress or pick another core drill).
You want dynamic rotation (use a woodchop).
common_errors: Letting the hips sag toward the floor
Rotating the torso forward or back
progressions: Hold a weight on the hip
